title: GSR 550 (E) The Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) (Amendment) Rules, 2015

Ereftare/1394/2009/3] Wa AYGar Ura, HreararsH faery, AraTara arftrsa (ez) fecroy- wot Fare aranr.ft.@. 570(a) ate 17 Hers, 2012 % art wrea & asa F varia feu ae yaar afeearar a. ar.ar.ft. 898(31) area 17 fedex, 2012 % ara atta are sonra Far ae a MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (RAILWAY BOARD) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 10th July, 2015 G.S.R. 550(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section(1) read with clause (d) of sub-section (2) of section 87 of the Railways Act, 1989(No. 24 of 1989), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Rules, 2012, namely:— 1. (1) These rules may be called the Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) (Amendment) Rules, 2015. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Railways(Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Rules, 2012, in the SCHEDULE,- (i) in Situation A, for part-II, the following part shall be substituted, namely:- “Part-II (i) For BCNHL wagons Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity and loading tolerance of half tonne If the weight of the commodity exceeds the permissible carrying capacity of wagon; -- 3 of 5 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(1)] (a) upto half tonne (b) by more than half tonne but not more than ; Two times of the freight rate applicable to that three and half tonnes commodity. (c) by more than three and half tonnes Three times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. *Explanation.- It is hereby clarified that on the weight exceeding the permissible carrying capacity upto half tonne, the normal freight at the rate applicable to the commodity shall be recoverable and punitive charges shall be recovered for the entire weight of the commodity in excess of permissible carrying capacity and loading tolerance of half tonne. (ii) For BCCW wagons =r Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity HS eee ———— If the weight of the commodity exceeds the permissible carrying capacity of wagon; (a) upto three tonnes Two times of the freight rate applicable to that commodity. | jp (b) By more than three tonnes Three times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. *Explanation.- Punitive charges shall be recovered for the entire weight of the commodity in excess of permissible carrying capacity.”; (ii) in Situation-B, for part-ll, the following part shall be substituted, namely:— “Part-II (i) For BCNHL wagons Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity If the weight of the commodity exceeds the permissible carrying capacity of wagon; ae — |(a) upto half tonne Nil —{—___—_— (b) by more than half tonne but not more than ; Three times of the freight rate applicable to the three and half tonnes highest class. SSS (c) by more than three and half tonnes Five times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. J *Explanation.- It is hereby clarified that on the weight exceeding the permissible carrying capacity upto half tonne, the normal freight at the rate applicable to the commodity shall be recoverable. However, in case of weight exceeds the permissible carrying capacity by more than half tonne, punitive charges shall be recovered for the entire weight of the commodity in excess of permissible carrying capacity. -- 4 of 5 -- [3M 1-@rEs 3(i)) Rd ST UTTT : TAT” 5 ee’ (ii) For BCCW wagons Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity If the weight of the commodity exceeds the permissible carrying capacity of wagon; (a) upto three tonnes (b) By more than three tonnes *Explanation.- Punitive charges shall be recovered for the entire weight of the commodity in excess of permissible carrying capacity.”. Three times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. Five times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. [F. N. TCR/1394/2009/3] N. MADHUSUDANA RAO, Executive Director, Traffic Comml. (Rates) Note.-The principal rules were published in the Gazette of India, vide number G.S.R. 570(E), dated the 17" July, 2012 and were last amended vide notification number G.S.R.898(E), dated the 17" December, 2012. 2036 6L)iS-2 Printed by the Manager, Government of India Press, Ring Road, Mayapuri, New Delhi-110064 and Published by the Controller of Publications, Delhi-1 10054. -- 5 of 5 --
